query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
## Capacity Threshold for the NYISO Large Generation Interconnection Process

The capacity threshold that triggers the **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** in NYISO is **20 MW**. Generating facilities that **exceed 20 MW** are subject to the large generation interconnection process.

### Sources and Specific Citations:

1. **NYISO Manual 23 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual (TEI Manual)**
   - **Section 3.3 — "Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)"** and **Section 3.3.1 — "Basic Information about the LFIP"** describe the large facility interconnection process, which is governed by **Attachment X** of the NYISO OATT.
   - Source: [Manual 23 (February 2025 version, v4.3)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/2924447/tei_manual.pdf/94a26e65-fd68-98e1-535b-fc41a9536607?t=1738941104360)

2. **NYISO OATT Attachment X (Section 30) — Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures**
   - The title of Attachment X itself explicitly states: **"Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities."**
   - **Section 30.3.1 (General)** further confirms: *"The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW..."*
   - Source: [OATT Section 30 — Attachment X](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%20Section%2030_HSR%20Revisions.pdf/9a47d1ba-7ee1-6789-f6b2-04acb82afa7d)

3. **NYISO UG-21 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide**
   - UG-21 (v3.0, effective 07/18/2025) provides a high-level summary of the interconnection procedures and references Manual 23 and the applicable OATT Attachments (including Attachment X for the legacy LFIP and the new Attachment HH for the Standard Interconnection Procedures). It directs readers to Manual 23 and the OATT for the governing thresholds and requirements.
   - Source: [UG-21 (v3.0, July 2025)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1768411358615)

### Important Note on Recent Changes:
As noted in the February 2025 version of Manual 23, on **May 1, 2024**, NYISO filed new **Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)** under **Attachment HH** of the OATT (in response to FERC Order No. 2023), which **replace** the former Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Attachment X) and Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (Attachment Z). The new SIP consolidates the process into a single cluster study framework. However, under the legacy LFIP framework described in Manual 23 Section 3.3 and Attachment X, the threshold was **20 MW**.

steps.content:
## Capacity Threshold for the NYISO Large Generation Interconnection Process

The capacity threshold that triggers the **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** in NYISO is **20 MW**. Generating facilities that **exceed 20 MW** are subject to the large generation interconnection process.

### Sources and Specific Citations:

1. **NYISO Manual 23 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual (TEI Manual)**
   - **Section 3.3 — "Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)"** and **Section 3.3.1 — "Basic Information about the LFIP"** describe the large facility interconnection process, which is governed by **Attachment X** of the NYISO OATT.
   - Source: [Manual 23 (February 2025 version, v4.3)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/2924447/tei_manual.pdf/94a26e65-fd68-98e1-535b-fc41a9536607?t=1738941104360)

2. **NYISO OATT Attachment X (Section 30) — Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures**
   - The title of Attachment X itself explicitly states: **"Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities."**
   - **Section 30.3.1 (General)** further confirms: *"The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW..."*
   - Source: [OATT Section 30 — Attachment X](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%20Section%2030_HSR%20Revisions.pdf/9a47d1ba-7ee1-6789-f6b2-04acb82afa7d)

3. **NYISO UG-21 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide**
   - UG-21 (v3.0, effective 07/18/2025) provides a high-level summary of the interconnection procedures and references Manual 23 and the applicable OATT Attachments (including Attachment X for the legacy LFIP and the new Attachment HH for the Standard Interconnection Procedures). It directs readers to Manual 23 and the OATT for the governing thresholds and requirements.
   - Source: [UG-21 (v3.0, July 2025)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1768411358615)

As noted in the February 2025 version of Manual 23, on **May 1, 2024**, NYISO filed new **Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)** under **Attachment HH** of the OATT (in response to FERC Order No. 2023), which **replace** the former Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Attachment X) and Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (Attachment Z). The new SIP consolidates the process into a single cluster study framework. However, under the legacy LFIP framework described in Manual 23 Section 3.3 and Attachment X, the threshold was **20 MW**.
steps.reasoning:
Based on my research, I have found clear and consistent evidence across multiple NYISO documents regarding the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process. Let me synthesize the findings:

1. **NYISO OATT Attachment X (Section 30)** - The title itself states: "Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"

2. **NYISO OATT Attachment X, Section 30.3.1** - States: "The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW..."

3. **Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual)** - Section 3.3 covers "Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)" and Section 3.3.1 covers "Basic Information about the LFIP." The manual references Attachment X of the OATT.

4. **UG-21 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide)** - This guide references Manual 23 and the OATT provisions, and describes the Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) applicable to generating facilities.

The threshold is clearly **20 MW** - generating facilities that exceed 20 MW are subject to the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) under Attachment X of the NYISO OATT, as described in Manual 23 (Section 3.3) and referenced in UG-21.

Note: The NYISO has recently (May 2024) filed new Standard Interconnection Procedures under Attachment HH that replace the old LFIP (Attachment X) and SGIP (Attachment Z), in response to FERC Order No. 2023. The new procedures consolidate the large and small generator processes. However, the historical threshold under the LFIP was 20 MW.
